title: fam-pow2 — 2^k / ldexp(1,k) for an integer exponent emits four-way as Form->asm bytes AND runs bit-exact on the M4; the exponent-ASSEMBLY step the native exp lane lacked, + the fa-lsl-x-imm shift encoder
author: Sema (Opus 4.8), autonomous native-ML walk
stone: form-native-models.form 'GAPS — LLM INFERENCE': the transcendental ops as form-asm bytes. exp(x) = 2^k * exp(r) with k = round(x*log2e) and r in [-ln2/2, ln2/2]. Every exp-lane PRIMITIVE was already on bytes — k=round (form-asm-frintn 7), the range reduction to r (form-asm-exp-reduce 31), and the Horner poly engine (form-asm-horner / exp-coef-pool 31) — EXCEPT the final 2^k scaling. fam-fmov-bits (#3933) proved the raw bit-reinterpret and fa-fmov-d-x's OWN comment named this exact brick ('build (k+1023)<<52 in an integer register, then fmov d,x'), but BUILDING the exponent field needed a runtime <<52 shift that had no encoder. This brick supplies it and composes the full 2^k. With it, exp's four moving parts (k, r, Horner, 2^k) are all sovereign native bytes; what remains is composing them into one fam-exp function.
new_encoder: fa-lsl-x-imm — arm64 logical-shift-left by a compile-time immediate, the LAST non-present primitive on the exp lane. arm64 has no dedicated LSL-immediate word; it is an alias of UBFM x<rd>,x<rn>,#(-shift MOD 64),#(63-shift): base 0xD3400000 | immr<<16 | imms<<10 | rn<<5 | rd, with immr = 64-shift and imms = 63-shift for 1<=shift<=63. The shift is DATA — ONE encoder for every amount (core-lift, no per-shift row), mirroring fa-movk-x's shift-as-data generalization. Byte-verified against the M4 assembler oracle (otool -tvVj of clang -c) at four points spanning the field and register space: lsl x0,x0,#52 == d34c2c00 (immr=12,imms=11), lsl x0,x0,#1 == d37ff800 (immr=63,imms=62), lsl x1,x2,#12 == d374cc41 (rd/rn vary), lsl x3,x5,#63 == d34100a3 (immr=1,imms=0, the boundary). The reusable win beyond 2^k: every bitfield shift (mantissa extraction, sign tests, fixed-point scaling) now has its native door.
recipe: form/form-stdlib/form-asm-matvec.fk :: (defn fam-pow2 () (append (fa-add-x-imm 0 0 1023) (append (fa-lsl-x-imm 0 0 52) (append (fa-fmov-d-x 0 0) (fa-ret))))). The key fact: an IEEE754 f64 with biased exponent field (k+1023) and zero mantissa IS exactly 2^k. fa-add-x-imm biases k, the new fa-lsl-x-imm shifts it <<52 into the exponent bits, fa-fmov-d-x REINTERPRETS the raw bits as an f64 (NOT fa-scvtf, which would numerically convert k's VALUE to a float — 5 -> 5.0, wrong for an exponent). ABI: x0 = k (signed int, -1022..1023 for a normal 2^k), returns d0 = 2^k. 4 arm64 instructions = 16 bytes.

proof_four_way.oracle: M4-assembled words (otool -tvVj of clang -c on the exact 4-instruction sequence for `double f(long k){union{unsigned long u;double d;}v; v.u=((unsigned long)(k+1023))<<52; return v.d;}`): 910ffc00 (add x0,x0,#1023) d34c2c00 (lsl x0,x0,#52) 9e670000 (fmov d0,x0) d65f03c0 (ret) = 16 bytes.
claims: 2 — the FULL 16-byte program byte-equals the M4-assembled oracle (fa-conviction == 1)
4 — lsl x0,x0,#52 (d34c2c00) — the NEW fa-lsl-x-imm, the <<52 exp-field shift, byte-exact (fa-conviction == 1)
8 — lsl x0,x0,#1 (d37ff800) — fa-lsl-x-imm generalizes to shift=1 (fa-conviction == 1)
16 — lsl x1,x2,#12 (d374cc41) — fa-lsl-x-imm generalizes across registers (fa-conviction == 1)
32 — lsl x3,x5,#63 (d34100a3) — fa-lsl-x-imm at the shift boundary (fa-conviction == 1)
proof_four_way.command: cd form && ./validate.sh form-stdlib/core.fk form-stdlib/format-arith.fk form-stdlib/form-asm.fk form-stdlib/form-asm-matvec.fk form-stdlib/tests/form-asm-pow2-band.fk
proof_four_way.result: -> 63 ; fourth arm four-way (fkwu, bootstrap binary darwin-arm64, no clang) ; 1 ok, 0 divergent — Go=Rust=TS=fkwu agree on the 16-byte image and on fa-lsl-x-imm at four shift/register points. Crossed FIRST try (loop-free, no forward-reference/recursion surface; the immr/imms arithmetic is compile-time-constant subtraction the four kernels compute identically).

proof_on_hardware.path: Form kernel emits fam-pow2's 16-byte program -> form-macho mo-object-sym -> Mach-O .o (249 bytes) exporting _recipe -> ld -dylib + ad-hoc sign -> dlopen+call. No rustc/clang in the COMPUTE (clang only builds the dumb dlopen harness; the exponent assembly is the Form-emitted bytes). otool of the .o confirms the words disassemble to the exact add/lsl/fmov/ret sequence (the assembler is the byte oracle).
proof_on_hardware.result: 12/12 MATCH on M4 (arm64 macOS): recipe(k) == ldexp(1.0, k) == 2^k bit-for-bit across k in {0,1,5,10,-1,-3,-10,52,127,-126,1023,-1022}, INCLUDING the normal-double exponent boundaries 2^1023 (8.9884656743115795e+307) and 2^-1022 (2.2250738585072014e-308) and 2^-126 (1.1754943508222875e-38). The <<52 shift places the biased exponent exactly, the bit-reinterpret reads it as the power of two, for every finite exponent.
proof_on_hardware.note: The last exp-lane primitive is now sovereign native bytes. 2^k is the scaling every exp/softmax/gelu multiplies its Horner(r) by (exp(x) = 2^k * exp(r)); with k=round (frintn), r (exp-reduce), and Horner (pool-fma) already on bytes, the whole exp range-reduction+reconstruction is now Form-emitted, running bit-exact on the M4, no rustc/clang in the compute.

no_regression: fam-pow2 is purely additive to form-asm-matvec.fk (a new recipe); fa-lsl-x-imm is purely additive to form-asm.fk (a new encoder, no existing word changed); the band and manifest row are additive. The scoped four-way validate loads form-asm.fk + form-asm-matvec.fk and crosses -> 63 with 0 divergent, re-exercising the sibling recipes' shared prelude. No byte in any existing recipe moved.
design_note: Design-with-reference held: the exact 4-instruction sequence AND the fa-lsl-x-imm word at four shift/register points were assembled with clang -c on THIS M4 and read back with otool BEFORE authoring, pinning every byte (the relu slip's lesson — four-way agreement proves DETERMINISM, not correctness; only the M4-assembled oracle proves correctness). My bit-derived UBFM base (0xD3400000 with immr=64-shift, imms=63-shift) matched the otool ground truth at all four points, so the shift-as-data generalization is proven, not assumed. READ-THE-BODY held: this brick was named in fa-fmov-d-x's OWN comment and in fam-fmov-bits' / fam-exp-reduce's receipts; fam-fmov-bits supplied the bit-reinterpret, and the only genuinely missing piece was the <<52 shift encoder. Uncontested-stone discipline (lane-5): checked git branch -r + git log origin/main.. first — the two most-recent claude branches (form-asm-softmax-max, form-asm-fmax-relu) were the source branches of already-merged bricks (#3939, #3937), not in-flight work; the exp-lane sibling (form-asm-exp-reduce) is merged; full-llama-generation (codex/full-real-llama-generation-d617) and Metal GPU dispatch are none of this brick's turf. 2^k is the atomic exp-lane primitive nobody was holding.
toolchain_free_claim: Honest floor: a Form recipe proven four-way (Go=Rust=TS=fkwu) through validate.sh (bash) PLUS an on-M4 execution witness (form-macho -> ld -dylib -> dlopen, no rustc/clang in the compute). bin-go is the bootstrap that runs the flattener/emitter. Platform rows (mac four-way + on-hardware observed; windows/android form-cli pending) per docs/coherence-substrate/standard-receipt.form.
next_stones: (1) fam-exp end-to-end on bytes: compose fam-exp-reduce (k and r) + fa-fcvtzs (k float -> int) + fam-pow2 (2^k) + a pooled-coefficient Horner exp(r), returning 2^k * exp(r), four-way + bit-exact vs libm exp — the full transcendental the softmax and gelu need. (2) fam-softmax over ll-buffer: fam-max-loop (m = max_j) -> subtract-exp(via fam-exp)-sum(fam-dot-loop)-normalize. (3) fam-gelu/fam-silu (exp + the pooled poly). The <<52 shift also unblocks native mantissa extraction / fixed-point scaling wherever a runtime bitfield shift is needed.
witness_state: pulse.coherencycoin.com at open: overall breathing, 0 recorded silences. Brick is host-independent (Form recipe + encoder + band + witness + receipt + manifest row); no deploy taken.
